def solve(l, r, directions)
    def is_good(x, directions)
        # 1. Format x to 16 digits string S
        s = x.to_s.rjust(16, '0')

        # 2. Populate 4x4 grid G
        # Initialize grid (4 rows, 4 columns)
        G = Array.new(4) { Array.new(4, 0) }
        
        for i in 0..15
            row = i / 4
            col = i % 4
            digit = s[i] - '0' # Convert character digit to integer
            G[row][col] = digit
        end

        # 3. Determine the path and record sequence P
        r = 0
        c = 0
        P = []
        
        # Start cell (Position 0)
        P << G[r][c] 
        
        # Apply 6 directions (Positions 1 through 6)
        for i in 0..5
            direction = directions[i]
            
            if direction == 'D'
                r += 1
            elsif direction == 'R'
                c += 1
            end
            
            # Record the digit at the new position
            # Note: Constraints guarantee r and c stay within bounds [0, 3]
            P << G[r][c]
        end
        
        # 4. Check for non-decreasing property
        # P has length 7. Check P[i] >= P[i-1] for i=1 to 6.
        is_non_decreasing = true
        (1...P.length).each do |i|
            if P[i] < P[i - 1]
                is_non_decreasing = false
                break
            end
        end
        
        is_non_decreasing
    end

    count = 0
    
    # Iterate through the range [l, r]
    x = l
    while x <= r
        if is_good(x, directions)
            count += 1
        end
        x += 1
    end
    
    count
end

                c_coord += 1

            # Record the digit at the new position
            P << G[r_coord][c_coord]
        end
        
        # 4. Check for non-decreasing property
        # P has length 7. Check P[i] >= P[i-1] for i=1 to 6.
        is_non_decreasing = true

            count += 1
        end
    end
